text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. In supervised learning, we give the computer a large set of training data. This data includes examples with the correct answers. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, it might predict whether an email is spam or not. After making predictions, we check the model's accuracy, which tells us how often it is correct. The goal is to improve the model until it is reliable and useful.
